title: cytisus hirsutus subsp. ratisbonensis
intro: Cytisus hirsutus subsp. ratisbonensis, also known as Ratisbon Spiny Broom, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the pea family, Fabaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its flavonoid and phenolic content, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. The Ratisbon Spiny Broom can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, making it a popular ingredient in traditional herbal remedies.
